UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: A profile representing a user of the OmniVid AI application, linked via Google authentication.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity.

googleId.description: The unique identifier provided by Google OAuth for this user.

profileImageUrl.description: URL to the user's profile picture, provided by Google.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was last updated.

Video.title: Video
Video.description: Represents an AI-generated video, including its metadata, status, and associated user.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Video entity.

userProfileId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who generated this video. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Video)

originalPrompt.description: The original text prompt provided by the user for video generation.

optimizedPrompt.description: The AI-optimized English prompt used by the video generation engine.

inputLanguage.description: The language in which the user provided the original prompt (e.g., 'en', 'hi', 'es').

outputLanguage.description: The target language for the video's voiceover, if applicable, derived from the input language or user choice.

visualStyle.description: The suggested or chosen visual style for the video (e.g., 'Cinematic', 'Anime', 'Realistic').

videoUrl.description: URL to the final generated video file stored in Google Cloud Storage.

thumbnailUrl.description: URL to a thumbnail image for the generated video.

status.description: The current generation status of the video (e.g., 'pending', 'processing', 'completed', 'failed', 'blocked').

resolution.description: The resolution of the generated video (e.g., '720p', '1080p').

queueType.description: Indicates if the video was generated via 'standard' or 'fast_pass' (ad-supported) queue.

durationSeconds.description: The duration of the generated video in seconds.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the video generation request was initiated.

completedAt.description: Timestamp when the video generation process was completed.

isWatermarked.description: Flag indicating whether the generated video includes an invisible digital watermark (for free tier).

isSafetyChecked.description: Flag indicating whether the video content has been processed and cleared by the content safety API.

definition.description: Stores user profile information. Each document's ID ({userId}) MUST be the Firebase Auth UID of the user. This document is private to its owner. Includes a denormalized 'id' field matching the {userId} for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique Firebase Auth User ID (UID) of the user, serving as the document ID and owner identifier.

definition.description: Stores details about an AI-generated video. Each video belongs to a specific user. The 'userProfileId' field is denormalized to match the parent {userId} for authorization independence and efficient rule evaluation. These documents are private to the owning user.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique Firebase Auth User ID (UID) of the user who owns this video.

params.name: videoId
params.description: The unique identifier for the video.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore structure prioritizes robust security, scalability, and debuggability by adhering to the core design principles of Authorization Independence, Structural Segregation, and consistent Access Modeling. The structure leverages hierarchical paths for user-owned data, effectively making the current user's authenticated ID (`request.auth.uid`) the primary authorization context.

**Authorization Independence:**
1.  **User Profiles:** User profiles are stored in `/user_profiles/{userId}`. The `userId` wildcard in the path directly corresponds to the Firebase Authentication UID (`request.auth.uid`). Furthermore, the `UserProfile` entity's `id` field will also store this `userId`. This setup ensures that accessing a user's profile (`get`, `update`, `delete`) only requires checking `request.auth.uid == userId` from the path, eliminating any need for `get()` operations on other documents for authorization. This enables atomic creation and updates of user profiles.
2.  **Videos:** Videos are nested under their respective user profiles in `/user_profiles/{userId}/videos/{videoId}`. The `Video` entity includes a `userProfileId` field, which is explicitly denormalized to match the `userId` in the parent path segment and `request.auth.uid`. For any operation on a `Video` document, security rules can directly check `request.auth.uid == userId` (from the path) and/or `request.auth.uid == resource.data.userProfileId`. This eliminates `get()` calls to the parent `UserProfile` document, ensuring authorization independence for video operations.

**Query as a Privilege (QAPs) Support:**
*   **Structural Segregation:** By nesting `Video` documents under `/user_profiles/{userId}/videos/{videoId}`, we create a homogeneous security posture for each subcollection. All videos within a specific `/user_profiles/{userId}/videos` subcollection belong to that `userId`. This means a `list` operation on `videos` is naturally scoped to the authenticated user's own videos by querying `db.collection('user_profiles').doc(request.auth.uid).collection('videos')`. There is no need for complex `where()` clauses or insecure collection group queries that would require filtering potentially unauthorized data on the client or within rules. The ownership is inherently enforced by the path, allowing secure and efficient `list` operations.
*   This structure ensures that users can only query their own data, and the server-side rules can enforce this ownership purely based on the request's authentication context and the document's inherent path or denormalized `userProfileId` field.
